differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Primary Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) presenting with catastrophic APS
Nephrotic syndrome with secondary hypercoagulability leading to bilateral renal vein thrombosis and pulmonary thromboembolism
Membranous nephropathy with secondary renal vein thrombosis
Infectious pneumonia with reactive thrombotic complications
next_steps: Initiate full therapeutic anticoagulation (e.g., heparin infusion)
Perform serum immunologic workup: ANA, dsDNA, complement levels (C3/C4), anti-phospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I)
Consult Nephrology for potential kidney biopsy to determine etiology of nephrotic syndrome
Consult Rheumatology for evaluation of systemic inflammatory disease
Obtain renal vein Doppler ultrasound or follow-up CT angiography if hemodynamically stable
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending blood/sputum culture results
Monitor for signs of Catastrophic Antiphospholipid Syndrome (CAPS) and organ failure
